name: Priya Chakraborty
chief_complaint: I have terrible pain in my upper stomach that goes straight through to my back, and I can't stop vomiting
onset_description: Severe epigastric pain began approximately 6 hours ago after a large family dinner. Pain is constant, boring in nature, and radiates to the back. Multiple episodes of vomiting. Pain is the worst she has ever experienced.

epigastric_pain.description: It's like a knife going straight through my stomach to my back. It's absolutely unbearable, I've never felt anything like this

vomiting.description: I've thrown up five or six times now, it's just bile at this point, nothing is staying down
aggravating_factors: any attempt to eat or drink

nausea.description: I feel sick to my stomach constantly, even between vomiting episodes

abdominal_distension.description: My belly feels bloated and tight, especially up top
abdominal_distension.character: diffuse bloating, worse in upper abdomen

labs.BMP: Na 136, K 3.6, Cr 1.0, Glucose 185 (elevated), BUN 20, Ca 8.4 (low-normal)
labs.liver_function: AST 110 (elevated), ALT 145 (elevated), Alk Phos 180 (elevated), Total Bilirubin 2.1 (elevated), Direct Bilirubin 1.4 (elevated), Albumin 3.5

past_medical_history: Cholelithiasis (gallstones diagnosed 2 years ago, declined surgery at the time)
medications: Calcium with Vitamin D supplement daily

social_history.alcohol: Rare, maybe a glass of wine at special occasions, 2-3 times per year
social_history.occupation: Software project manager, works from home
social_history.living_situation: Married, lives with husband and two children ages 10 and 13
social_history.exercise: Tries to walk 30 minutes daily, does yoga twice a week
social_history.diet: Mostly home-cooked Indian food. Admits the family dinner last night was very rich and heavy with fried foods.

conditions.father: Type 2 Diabetes, gallstones, died at 70 from MI

personality: articulate and well-informed, asks detailed questions about her care, very anxious about her children and work obligations
communication_style: Educated and precise in describing symptoms. Uses some medical terminology from her own research. Asks 'what does that mean?' when given lab results. Frequently mentions her kids and husband waiting in the lobby. Wants to understand the plan.
pain_description_style: Very descriptive and specific about pain quality, location, and timeline. Rates pain accurately. Says things like 'it's a deep, boring pain that radiates straight through to my back, worse when I lie flat.' Does not exaggerate or minimize.

critical_findings: Markedly elevated lipase (1850 U/L) confirming acute pancreatitis
Known gallstone history with elevated liver enzymes and bilirubin suggesting gallstone pancreatitis
Classic presentation: epigastric pain radiating to back, worse lying flat, better leaning forward
Tachycardia and low-grade fever suggesting inflammatory response
Elevated glucose in known diabetic may indicate pancreatic endocrine dysfunction
Elevated direct bilirubin and hepatic enzymes suggesting common bile duct obstruction
Morphine allergy - important for pain management planning
History of known gallstones with prior refusal of cholecystectomy
recommended_interventions: Aggressive IV fluid resuscitation (Lactated Ringer's at 250-500mL/hr initially)
IV analgesics - hydromorphone or fentanyl (avoid morphine per allergy)
Right upper quadrant ultrasound to evaluate for CBD dilation and gallstones
MRCP or EUS if CBD stone suspected
GI/surgery consult for likely cholecystectomy during same admission
Monitor glucose closely with insulin sliding scale
Serial lipase and liver function tests
Assess BISAP or Ranson's criteria for severity scoring
